name: Adaptive Epsilon Tuning
overview: TrustDB dynamically tunes the B-epsilon tree's epsilon parameter and buffer flush thresholds at runtime based on workload characteristics. The optimal configuration (threshold=8192, epsilon=0.8) achieves 8,616 ops/sec, but the system adapts these values as the read/write ratio and data distribution change. This eliminates the need for manual tree parameter tuning.

howItWorks.title: Workload Monitoring
howItWorks.description: A lightweight statistics collector tracks the read/write ratio, average write size, buffer fill rates, and flush frequency over a sliding window. These metrics are updated atomically with minimal overhead.

howItWorks.title: Epsilon Adjustment
howItWorks.description: The epsilon parameter (controlling the write-buffer-to-fanout tradeoff) is adjusted based on the read/write ratio. Write-heavy workloads increase epsilon (larger buffers, fewer flushes); read-heavy workloads decrease epsilon (smaller buffers, faster point lookups).

howItWorks.title: Threshold Tuning
howItWorks.description: The buffer flush threshold is tuned based on average write size and available memory. Larger writes benefit from lower thresholds (more frequent, smaller flushes); smaller writes benefit from higher thresholds (batched flushes).

howItWorks.title: Convergence
howItWorks.description: Adjustments use exponential moving averages to avoid oscillation. The system converges to stable parameters within ~10 seconds of a workload change and re-adjusts if the workload shifts again.

comparisons.source: Default parameters not tuned for workload
LSM-tree; different write amplification profile; higher throughput for simple KV

comparisons.narrative: RocksDB offers extensive tuning knobs (memtable size, L0 file count, compaction strategy) and some auto-tuning via dynamic level size. TrustDB's adaptive epsilon is narrower in scope (B-epsilon-specific parameters) but fully automatic. The 8,616 ops/sec peak is lower than RocksDB's throughput for simple KV workloads, but TrustDB's operations include governance overhead (confidence, clearance, provenance) that RocksDB operations do not carry.

competitors.approach: Manual tuning with 100+ configuration options; some auto-tuning for level sizes
competitors.limitation: Requires expert tuning for optimal performance; auto-tuning limited to level sizing

competitors.approach: Configurable cache size, checkpoint interval, compaction
competitors.limitation: Manual tuning; no runtime adaptation to workload changes

competitors.approach: Minimal tuning (map size only); B-tree with fixed structure
competitors.limitation: No adaptive tuning possible; fixed B-tree fanout
No runtime tuning; fixed LSM configuration
